item.text: I found one correctness gap the earlier note understated: `applyEdit` only auto-rebuilds clip order for deletions, not brand-new clip IDs. I’m checking the clip-order helper now so the replacement clip lands in persisted order instead of silently depending on row array order.

I’ve confirmed the editor has one subtle gap the earlier pseudocode missed: new clip IDs need an explicit `clipOrderOverride` if we want persistence order to stay correct. I’m implementing the switch action with direct asset registration plus a targeted clip-order replacement so the new final-video clip lands where the image group used to be.

The main switch path is written. I’m doing a source pass over the new callback now, specifically to make sure the asset key, registry entry, row mutation, and clip-order override all line up with SC3 rather than just “mostly working”.

There’s one more guard worth adding before I close this batch: if the row disappeared between opening the menu and applying the edit, the current code would delete the old clips without inserting the replacement. I’m adding that row-exists bail-out now, then I’ll checkpoint SC3.

The row-exists guard is in. I’m doing the last verification pass now on the exact SC3 points: `generateUUID`, `getNextClipId`, the `video/mp4` registry entry, and the `applyEdit({ type: 'rows', metaDeletes: clipIds, ... })` call.
